description_long: This is a weathered wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing at the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held together by three prominent dark metal hoops encircling the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom. The wood appears aged, with a grayish tone and visible grain, knots, and some darkened spots, suggesting exposure to the elements. The interior is open and hollow, indicating the barrel is empty and capable of holding contents. The rim is slightly beveled, and there are no visible handles or lids. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near garages, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered, gray wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and an open top, showing signs of age and outdoor exposure. The barrel is cylindrical and hollow, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and two visible metal hoops with a knot and dark spot on the staves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the continuity of the metal hoops, with less visible wood markings.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear view of the open top and a different arrangement of knots and dark spots on the wood.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the same structural features with slightly less pronounced wood grain and markings.
